skill.name: environment-setup-guide
skill.description: Guide developers through setting up development environments with proper tools, dependencies, and configurations
skill.summary: Step-by-step guide for installing tools, configuring dependencies, and verifying development environments
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation content providing development environment setup instructions. The static scanner flagged example shell commands and configuration examples as potential security issues, but these are standard instructional examples for installing software and configuring environments. No actual code execution or malicious patterns present - all findings are false positives from a documentation file.

high_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive: External Commands
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged 114 'external_commands' locations, but these are example shell commands in documentation (brew install, apt update, npm install). Not executable malicious code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is a markdown documentation file with example commands. The skill provides step-by-step setup instructions, not executable code that runs on the user's system.

high_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive: Network Access
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged network URLs as security concerns. These are legitimate official download URLs for software installation (Homebrew, Node.js, Python).
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to official sources like nodejs.org, python.org, docker.com - standard legitimate software distribution endpoints.

high_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive: Filesystem Access
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged hidden files (.bashrc, .zshrc, .npmrc, .env) as security concerns. These are standard developer configuration files documented for setup purposes.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation explains how to configure standard development environment files - common practice in developer onboarding.

high_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive: Environment Access
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged .env file references as sensitive. This is standard documentation for setting up environment variables.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Skill shows users how to create .env files with placeholder values - legitimate developer guidance.

high_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positive: Critical Heuristic
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'dangerous combination: code execution + network + credentials' as critical. This is a documentation skill with example commands, not actual execution.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill provides markdown documentation with example commands. Claude does not automatically execute these - they are instructional content for users to read and optionally run themselves.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers set up complete development environments from scratch with step-by-step guidance for installing tools, configuring dependencies, and verifying everything works correctly.
actual_capabilities: Provide platform-specific installation instructions for macOS, Linux, and Windows
Guide users through checking existing tool versions before installation
Show how to configure environment variables and configuration files
Offer verification steps to confirm successful installation
Include troubleshooting for common development environment issues
Provide setup script templates for automation
limitations: Does not automatically install software - provides guidance only
Does not execute commands on the user's system
Does not modify system configuration without user action
Does not provide interactive step-by-step installation

use_cases.title: New Developer Onboarding
use_cases.description: Help new team members set up their development environment with all required tools and dependencies

use_cases.title: Machine Migration
use_cases.description: Guide users through recreating their development environment on a new machine or operating system

use_cases.title: Project Setup Documentation
use_cases.description: Create clear setup instructions for team members to follow when starting a new project

prompt_templates.title: Basic Environment Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a development environment for a Node.js project. I need to install Node.js, npm, Git, and Docker.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to set up basic tools for web development

prompt_templates.title: Python Environment
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to set up a Python development environment with Python 3.11, pip, virtual environments, and common packages like Flask and SQLAlchemy.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to set up Python-specific development environment

prompt_templates.title: Database Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up PostgreSQL and Redis for local development. Include configuration for environment variables and connection testing.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs database systems for their development stack

prompt_templates.title: Full Stack Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete development environment setup guide for a MERN stack application. Include Node.js, MongoDB, React, and all required environment variables.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to set up a full-stack development environment

output_examples.input: Help me set up a Node.js development environment on macOS
output: ## Node.js Development Environment Setup for macOS
Run this command in your terminal:

output_examples.input: What environment variables do I need for a Flask app?
output: Create a .env file with these variables:
- Never commit .env files to version control
- Use strong random values for SECRET_KEY

best_practices: Always check existing tool versions before installing new ones to avoid conflicts
Use version managers like nvm for Node.js and pyenv for Python to manage multiple versions
Document all required environment variables using .env.example files
anti_patterns: Don't use sudo with npm install as it can cause permission issues
Don't skip environment variable configuration - always document required variables
Don't assume all team members have the same operating system - provide platform-specific instructions

faq.question: Does this skill install software on my computer?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides documentation and guidance. It shows you the commands to run, but you must execute them yourself in your terminal.

faq.question: Can this help me set up Docker?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes detailed instructions for installing Docker on macOS, Linux, and Windows, plus docker-compose setup examples.

faq.question: What programming languages does this cover?
faq.answer: The skill provides examples for Node.js, Python, Go, and general development environment setup. It includes platform-specific instructions for all major operating systems.

faq.question: How do I troubleshoot 'command not found' errors?
faq.answer: The skill includes a troubleshooting section covering common issues like PATH configuration, shell sourcing, and permission fixes.

faq.question: Can I use this for team onboarding?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is ideal for creating documentation to help new team members set up their development environments quickly.

faq.question: Does this work on Windows?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes Windows-specific instructions using Chocolatey and direct installers for all major tools.
